expectations.text: Distinguishes group tables (many-to-one aggregation: many rows of one analysis bundled into one downstream-facing key) from merge tables (interchangeable versions of one analysis behind a single `merge_id`) — these are different patterns, not synonyms
expectations.evidence: Section 'Group tables are not merge tables' with explicit comparison table: 'Merge = I don't care which version... Group = I want this specific named bundle.'

expectations.text: Lists the shared shape: master with group-name PK + a `dj.Part` table keyed on the upstream rows the group includes (typically merge IDs via `.proj()` to rename)
expectations.evidence: States: 'master = -> Session + a user-named varchar in the PK, part = -> master + -> SomeUpstream.proj(<name>=merge_id)' and explains the .proj rename rationale.

expectations.text: Explains that `UnitSelectionParams` is a parameter table, NOT a group table itself — it FK-feeds into `SortedSpikesGroup`'s PK as the parameter axis but isn't the same kind of object
expectations.evidence: Section 'UnitSelectionParams is not a group table': 'It just stores label-filter rows... its role is parameters, not many-to-one aggregation. Different kind of object.'

expectations.text: Names at least `SortedSpikesGroup` and `PositionGroup` as the two concrete examples in source
expectations.evidence: Names both with file references: 'SortedSpikesGroup — src/spyglass/spikesorting/analysis/v1/group.py:63-74' and 'PositionGroup — src/spyglass/decoding/v1/core.py:130-143'.
